event: emergently brought to Nephrology, Dialysis and Transplantation Unit
lying on the floor throughout the night
traces of blood in bladder catheter
blood cultures showed growth of E. coli resistant to piperacillin/tazobactam
significant reduction in the levels of myoglobin
endogenous ultrafiltrate flow obtained per session
spite of the normalization of myoglobin
right jugular central venous catheter was placed
urine output was 2,300 mL per day
serum levels of urea dropped to 96 mg/dL
white blood cells count to 6,300 cells/µL
after 6 months from the last dialysis session
